releases.name: v26.0.0 - Revision 8 + super beta 8xx support
releases.body:
### **UPDATE 2**: Added working a8xx Turnip. **Please read the notes below.**  - Thanks whbexium
### **UPDATE**: Fixed an issue with the drivers not loading properly.

## Based on latest mesa main source v26.0.0-devel.

## v26.0.0 R8
Vulkan 1.4.335
Base commit:  e9a83d530cce

### NOTES

## The a8xx support included is **very** beta and hacky. **Use at your own risk!**  
An example of what might happen (which happened to me) using this with Mincraft Pojav-glow-worm on a840, it will crash and the default driver won't recover causing every apps to crash afterwards. (a reboot fixed it) 

What works very well: OpenGL games through x86_64 emulators.
What does not work: Switch emulators and non OpenGL games (so far tested. They boot but render very buggy graphics).

---

- This still uses Old autotuner. If you need new Autotuner, [use v25.3.0 here](https://github.com/K11MCH1/AdrenoToolsDrivers/releases/edit/v26.0.0-rc.03).

- a710 and 720 can use regular or Gmem. When using regular driver on Winlator, do uncheck sysmem and check gmem from the TU_DEBUG list in the Environment Variables. For other emulators, use Gmem driver.
- For users with the ONE UI bug, use the following environmental variable: `FD_DEV_FEATURES = enable_tp_ubwc_flag_hint=1`

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

### APPLIED PATCHES

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R8_Gmem.zip**
- Added a710/720 GPU descriptors (hacks)

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R8_Autotuner.zip**
- Autotuner Overhaul

**a8xx_0x5_handle.zip**
- Added a830v2 GPU descriptor (hack)
- Added 0x5 handle (hack)
Thanks Hardware Union RU/ENG boys

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Rob Clark
Karmjit Mahil
Connor Abbott
StevenMX
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

releases.name: v26.0.0 - Revision 3 + v25.3.0 with NEW Autotuner
releases.body:
## Based on latest mesa main source v26.0.0-devel.

## v26.0.0 R3
Vulkan 1.4.330
Base commit: 13148afd0

## v25.3.0
Vulkan 14.328
Base commit: 13148afd0
Repo: [PixelyIon/mesa/-/tree/tu-newat](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/PixelyIon/mesa/-/tree/tu-newat)

- Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
- Compatible with Micewine.  

---

### Notes  

- `Turnip v25.3.0 PREFER_SYSMEM/PROFILED` contains the new Autotuner that is not yet merged to main source. For testing purposes.
- The Turnip drivers above may not work for a710/720 as forcing Gmem is not possible on them.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3` contains old autotuner and gpu a710/720 hacks. Has a perf regression which is fixed with the ones below.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix` contains old autotuner, a perf fix and gpu hacks.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Gmem` contains the perf fix, gpu hacks and forced Gmem for a710/720.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Sysmem` contains the perf fix, gpu hacks and forced Sysmem.
- a710 and 720 can use regular or Gmem. When using regular driver on Winlator, do uncheck `sysmem` and check `gmem` from the TU_DEBUG list in the Environment Variables. For other emulators, use Gmem driver.

What is Autotuner

Automatically benchmarks each shader using both sysmem (system memory) and gmem (GPU memory) rendering paths. Over multiple render passes, it measures the execution time of each mode and determines which one consistently performs better. As it gathers more data, it gradually favors the faster mode for that shader, improving performance dynamically in real time. Depending on the workload, it may offer better performance.

**Currently v26.0.0 has the old Autotuner.**

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

### APPLIED PATCHES

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix:** 
- a710/720 support hack
[Fix CB overhead when running without CB being used.](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/38378)

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Gmem:** 
- As above + Force Gmem.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Connor Abbott
StevenMX
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

## Based on latest mesa main source v25.2.0-devel.

Vulkan 1.4.318

Base commit: 7544aae77c

### Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
### Compatible with Micewine.

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

> [!NOTE]
> <samp> - May not work with DXVK 2.6 and up.  </samp>
> <samp> - `GMEM` for a710 and a720 Winlator users.  </samp>  
> <samp> - `AUTO` for anyone else. However might still need to enable `SYSMEM` on Winlator/Micewine via `TU_DEBUG`</samp>  

> **If full screen artifacing happens, try using the [gmem] tagged driver.**

---

<details>

<summary>📜 What is Autotuner:</summary>

The Autotuner in Mesa's Turnip drivers dynamically selects between GMEM (tile-based, on-chip memory) and sysmem (direct system memory rendering) modes to optimize performance for Adreno GPUs. It uses heuristics to make decisions but can be overridden with TU_DEBUG options for debugging or to address specific issues. While effective in many cases, the Autotuner may struggle with certain workloads or newer GPUs, requiring manual configuration or updated driver variants for optimal performance. Which is why the unsupported GPUs mentioned above may need GMEM.

</details>

---

### MERGED PATCHES

- Hacky a710 and a720.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

Based on latest mesa main source v25.2.0-devel.

Vulkan 1.4.318

Base commit: eecf94cc

### Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
### Compatible with Micewine.

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- Bumped Vulkan version to 1.4.318.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

> [!NOTE]
> <samp> - `GMEM` for a710 and a720 Winlator users.  </samp>  
> <samp> - `AUTO` for anyone else. However might still need to enable `SYSMEM` on Winlator/Micewine via `TU_DEBUG`</samp>  

> **If full screen artifacing happens, try using the [gmem] tagged driver.**

---

<details>

<summary>📜 What is Autotuner:</summary>

The Autotuner in Mesa's Turnip drivers dynamically selects between GMEM (tile-based, on-chip memory) and sysmem (direct system memory rendering) modes to optimize performance for Adreno GPUs. It uses heuristics to make decisions but can be overridden with TU_DEBUG options for debugging or to address specific issues. While effective in many cases, the Autotuner may struggle with certain workloads or newer GPUs, requiring manual configuration or updated driver variants for optimal performance. Which is why the unsupported GPUs mentioned above may need GMEM.

</details>

---

### MERGED PATCHES

- Hacky a710 and a720.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

GameHub Lite v5.3.3 RC-1 [PRE-RELEASE]

This is an experimental build that adds **Micro SD Card Support** for **Steam Games** based on GameHub v5.3.3.

There is no support for this build, this is meant for those who are helping out or want to tinker.

**IMPORTANT:**

- Enable SD Card support in the settings before downloading Steam games
- Switching storage location back and forth hasn’t been tested yet.
- It will create a directory named `GHL` on your SD Card where the downloaded Steam games will be stored. (FF modders, go wild).
- This version can be installed alongside GHL v5.1.3 for easy testing.
- The full Steam client doesn’t work in this version, use Steam Lightweight or use GameHub Lite 5.1.3 which is stable.

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0 Patch
releases.body:
# Patch & Patch Generation (GameHub v5.1.0)

The files needed to contribute to the project and patch GameHub 5.1.0 yourself are now released. 

- Completely reworked the initial patching approach.
- *Revanced* patch is a work in progress, feel free to contribute to this if you are comfortable with revanced. 
- Easy update patches after modifications 
- Work on macOS/Linux. (maybe windows?)

Read the [README](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/blob/master/README.md) file for a full explanation.

### FAQ

- Does this work with GameHub version *not 5.1.0*
- **no.**


--- 


## How It Works

The patcher uses a multi-step process:

1. **Decompile** - Uses apktool to decompile the original APK to smali bytecode
2. **Delete** - Removes telemetry SDKs, unused assets, and tracking libraries
3. **Patch** - Applies unified diff patches to modify smali code
4. **Add** - Copies new files (resources, additional smali)
5. **Rebuild** - Reassembles the APK using apktool
6. **Sign** - Signs with a debug keystore for installation

## Patch Contents

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Deletions | 3,385 | Telemetry SDKs, tracking code, unused assets |
| Additions | 2,856 | New resources, modified assets, new features |
| Modifications | 223 | Smali code patches for behavior changes |

### Removed Components

- **Native libraries**: libumeng-spy.so, libcrashsdk.so, libalicomphonenumberauthsdk_core.so, etc.
- **SDKs**: Umeng Analytics, JPush, Firebase Analytics, Tencent login
- **Assets**: Splash video, auth videos, emoji font (saves ~30MB)
- **Permissions**: Location, contacts, phone state, ad tracking

### Added Features

- Custom splash/intro video from community CDN
- Local game ID copy functionality
- Offline mode improvements
- News page integration with community API

## For Developers

### Regenerating Patches

If you've modified the Lite APK and want to update the patches:

```bash
./generate-patches.sh [path/to/original.apk] [path/to/lite.apk]
```

This will:
1. Decompile both APKs
2. Generate diff patches for modified files
3. Copy new files to patches directory
4. Create deletion and addition lists

### Patch Directory Structure

```
patches/
├── files_to_delete.txt        # List of files to remove
├── files_to_add.txt             # List of files to add
├── files_to_patch.txt          # List of files to modify
├── diffs/                                # Unified diff patches
│   ├── AndroidManifest.xml.patch
│   ├── smali/...
│   └── res/...
├── new_files/                        # New files to add
│   ├── res/...
│   └── smali_classes10/...     # Robot language files to add
└── stats.txt                           # Patch statistics (ignored and generated on patch-generation)
```

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0
releases.body:
A restoration build of GameHub Lite V4 before everything fell apart. I will publish the code for the workers and a patch very soon, for now enjoy a working GameHub Lite app again.

We'll focus on restoring the previous functionality first, before fixing and adding functionality. So please only report issues that were not present in GameHub Lite V4 or in the official GameHub app.

---

### Explanation for the different versions

You’ll often see an emulator distribute different versions with a name suffixed like **PUBG**, **Antutu**, **Optimized**, **Genshin**, **Ludashi** and other names. Here follows an explanation for what is what, is still has slight differences between manufacturers and even different packagenames but it should give you a rough understanding.

—-

*The different versions are identical. What we do is a very old android trick to gain extra performance on some devices.*

**Antutu**

Some manufacturers “cheat” by setting the governor to performance when they detect the Antutu package name. 

*Nerd explanation:*

The CPU governor essentially controls the CPU's frequency scaling. allowing it to operate at different clock speeds and voltages based on the system load. So making the CPU go fast for sustained usage, what is actually made for peak usage. This comes with a risk of overheating, but I don’t believe in this. Android does a well enough job of thermal management and makes it extremely hard for software to exceed what the hardware is capable of and damaging itself.


That said, it’s still extra heat. More heat == more bad. I just think it’s negligible, especially if your device has a fan.


**PUBG**

On a high level it’s the same as Antutu, but some slight differences that only benefit games. Think of network prioritization and touch input latency improvements. The manufacturers goal when they detect Antutu is **ALL THE POWER**. Benchmarks are relatively short and it makes them look better on comparison websites.


The goal for PUBG is more like **MORE POWER**, since the intention is often to have a game running for longer it has less aggressive changes.


**TLDR and summary:**

**Antutu** spoofing:

    •    Maximum CPU/GPU frequencies unlocked  
    •    Aggressive performance governors  
    •    Short-duration performance boost (benchmark workload)  
    •    Thermal limits are less strict   
    •    All cores available  


**PUBG** and other games spoofing: 

    •    Sustained gaming performance profiles  
    •    GPU driver optimizations (Adreno/Mali game-specific paths)  
    •    Frame pacing and scheduling improvements  
    •    Reduced touch latency   
    •    Network QoS prioritization  
    •    Different thermal management (sustained vs burst)  
    •    Qualcomm “Game Performance Mode”      
    •    Sometimes enables features like frame-gen


**Ludashi** spoofing:

    •    Similar to Antutu but slightly less aggressive    
    •    Longer sustained performance boost (multi-minute tests)   
    •    Memory frequency optimization

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.0.0
releases.body:
This is a temporary release that restores most of the functionality previously available in GameHub Lite v4.

GameHub Lite v4 stopped working after the former developer took down the Cloudflare Workers it relied on, citing privacy concerns. 

We are currently developing a community-funded solution to provide a more stable and permanent setup.

In the meantime, this version includes most of the core features but may display some visual inconsistencies and lacks certain extras that were present in v4, such as the news and free games sections.

Special thanks to **The412Banner** and **Trixarian**!

[Join the EmuReady Discord for support and more information](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav)

releases.name: v0.8.1
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Added Steam achievement support!
- Correctly install prerequisites like VCRedist, PhysX, XNA, etc by @unbelievableflavour
- Add quick menu performance HUD overlay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/777
- Fix bug for Steam downloads where they get stuck on "Resume" in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/767
- Fix local saves being overwritten with cloud saves if connection lost during gameplay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/791
- Fixed infinite booting screens, black screens on boot and improved ability to download contents from our endpoints
- Fixes Edit Container crashes on older Android versions by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
- Fix external game launches while app is open & avoid pop-ups on external launch by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/759
- Added container config export/import for all platforms by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/649
- Added per-game auto-pause/resume settings by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/746
- Fixed VirGL in Glibc containers - Will enable Mali users to play 2D games in Glibc by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/763
- Fixed touchpad input by @AndreVto in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/725
- Added language-support for gen 1 GOG downloader by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/743
- Added language support for Epic (also shrinks game sizes) by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/754
- Replace Toast with Compose Snackbar system by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/697
- Show games on internal and external storage at the same time by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/781
- Allow searching for game titles with diacritics removed by @danbruno in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
- add fexcore 2603 by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/723
- remove trim for passwords to fix trailing spaces in some user passwords by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/736
- Fix: Closing FileInputStream and BufferedReader on listRunningWineProcesses by @giovannygb in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756
- refactor: deduplicate network connectivity checks by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/648
- Fix app crash in race condition in DownloadInfo ETA by snapshotting speed samples by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/755
- fix: resume immediately when opening keyboard in manual suspend mode by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/779
- Translation into Russian by @Denis11212 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695

Bugfixes from v0.8.1-prerelease:
* fix: skip achievements generation when Steam not logged in by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/835
* fix: allow intent launches for non-Steam and offline-mode games by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/832
* Added app screen gradient so back button stays visible by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/843
* Fix/cpu affinity empty selection crash by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/764
* fix: wait for exit sync before returning to launcher by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/792
* fix(): Epic name mismatch between game and container ID + added logging for container deletion to catch orphans by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/760
* Fix crash on empty depot list (#729) by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/772
* fix: update action shows Resume instead of download progress by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/806
* Updated achievement uploading code to not skip achievements.json when… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/847
* Cleaned up UI on Portrait screens (on game page) and on small landsca… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/862
* Consolidated networking code to always attempt Google DNS, should pre… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/861
* Handle XServerView lifecycle across app sleep by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/853
* fix: SD card games unrecoverable when external storage toggle off by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/809

## New Contributors
* @Denis11212 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695
* @danbruno made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
* @Catpotatos made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
* @giovannygb made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.8.0...v0.8.1

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git_3
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git_3  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A830/A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes:
- Import upstream as usual
- Decrease reg_size on 830 to 96 - should fix Cyberpunk 2077 crashes. See the commit info.
- Integrate [unmerged](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/39751) KGSL timeline sync MR - works good enough now. This is a proper replacement for old cringy vibecoded timeline sync emulation code I removed few releases ago. 
- Remove gmem cache offset completely as it breaks on lower-end A8XX GPUs.
- due to that, change cache sizes for A825/A810. GMEM should work on A825 now.
- Integrate zdobersek's patch fixing (workaround'ing) FLUSHALL. There's one build now as flushall is disabled by default and fixed.

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes compared to v23:
* Upstream Mesa
* Now doing new builds by applying my patches up on upstream Mesa-git branch instead of cloning my repo (I have updated it as well)
* Removed vibecoded timeline sync emulation shit and other unneeded hacks (read the commit message before blaming me for using AI AI AI)
* Started doing flushall & noflushall builds - flushall is still enabled for A8XX for various reasons
* Various changes in freedreno/tu from Mesa (still no proper A830 config)
* Disabled thin LTO and LTO in general (apparently causes weird bugs in RADV, might also apply for Turnip)
* Maybe something else...

Didn't test this release on my device, but should work well.  

I've also seen interesting Turnip builds with some "cool" fixes, but neither me interested in looking into patches (in sense of asking for sources or searching the repo) nor the author wants to share `git format-patch` of their fork (FWIW I shared all my patches in specific Telegram group), so I care less.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v23
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V23  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready
          
Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working, less stable than all other GPUs) - sysmem only
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

This is a build of current upstream Mesa (26.0) with my UBWC hacks applied.  
Unlike my old Turnip builds, this one is based directly on the main repo with only essential hacks applied to boot it on Android WSI platform. As I said, it won't work on GPUs other than Adreno 840.

releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V20  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working at least in TestD3D) - might be sysmem-only, didn't test gmem
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:  
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found). It works fine with drm-shim, so maybe same for real devices?

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Additionally A810 cache size is awfully small, so don't expect magic performance on Turnip.

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Import upstream changes
* Add proper initial support for Adreno 810. It's now rendering a cube!  
* Add support for A710/A720. These were full copies of A730 with only different ids in termux mesa patches.

The issue with A810 is its very small gmem cache (576KB). This caused an integer underflow, because nobody thought of A810 and its so-small cache, breaking rendering completely as invalid value was written onto `RB_CCU_CACHE_CNTL` register. I have discovered this with a debug build of Mesa, it was hitting an assert even with drm-shim due to this underflow.

releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan Vgralloc-exp (V14)

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* ~~gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* ~~Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub~~
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Added a workaround to fix u_gralloc skipping setting drm format modifier type - all weird pixel artifacts without WRAPPER_BLIT=1 should be gone now!

More info:
Fallback gralloc implementation in Mesa has this check to set proper drm format modifier for ubwc/nonubwc swapchain images (ubwc is a Qualcomm compression):
```c
   if (hnd->handle->numInts >= 2 && hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds] == gmsm) {
      /* This UBWC flag was introduced in a5xx. */
      bool ubwc = hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds + 1] & 0x08000000;
      out->modifier = ubwc ? DRM_FORMAT_MOD_QCOM_COMPRESSED : DRM_FORMAT_MOD_LINEAR;
   }
```
Looks like a8xx changed something here thus this condition fails and drm format isn't being set at all. This was the reason of garbled output in most native Vulkan apps.  
Removing the check is enough to overcome this issue.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v13
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V13

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
~~* gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Disabled GMEM on A830/A810 - these GPUs have broken GMEM configuration causing GPU hangs in the default usage (i.e. without sysmem debug variable). This change allows the driver to work on A830/A810 without mentioned variable.
* Stopped building yuck branch builds - I'm yet to see real improvement of reverting useful commits.

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v2.0
releases.body:
# 🎮 GameHub Brasil v2.0 🇧🇷

## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão O GameHub Brasil v2.0 chega para revolucionar como você gerencia seus jogos e drivers. Focamos totalmente na flexibilidade e compatibilidade, permitindo que você importe seus próprios arquivos, gerencie perfis de controle com facilidade e leve a experiência de jogos para dispositivos de qualquer gama de desempenho. --- 🚀 Novidades e Recursos Implementados --- ## 📥AdrenoHub Drivers (Adrenotools). ✅ Integração Adrenotools: Sistema robusto para gerenciamento de drivers Vulkan. 📂 Importação Flexível: Suporte para importação de drivers Turnip no formato .zip. 💾 Fontes Variadas: Carregue drivers de fontes externas ou diretamente do armazenamento local do seu dispositivo. ## 📂 Contents Hub Centralize seus conteúdos de forma prática e rápida. 🎮 Formato .wcp: Importação completa de Contents no formato proprietário. 🔄 Compatibilidade Híbrida: Suporte a arquivos externos e locais. ⚠️ Nota Técnica: O importador de conteúdo está funcional, porém, a importação do **Wine** ainda enfrenta limitações devido a diferenças de arquitetura, estando em aperfeiçoamento. ## 🕹️ Personalização de Controle Jogue do seu jeito, sem precisar configurar tudo do zero. 📥 Importação de Perfis: Adicione perfis de controle personalizados para jogos específicos. 🌐 Fontes Externas: Compatibilidade com perfis baixados da comunidade. 🎯 Praticidade: Aplicação rápida para agilizar o setup dos seus jogos favoritos. ## 💾 Suporte a Armazenamento Externo (OTG) Mais espaço e liberdade para sua biblioteca de jogos. 🔌 Reconhecimento OTG: O app agora detecta e gerencia perfeitamente pen drives e HDs externos conectados via OTG. 📂 Jogar Externo: Instale e rode seus jogos diretamente do armazenamento removível. ## ⚡ LowSpecsExperienceV13 Leve jogos pesados para hardwares mais modestos. 📉 Otimização Gráfica: Programa dedicado a reduzir e otimizar jogos. 🎮 Foco em Jogabilidade: Prioriza FPS e estabilidade em detrimento de efeitos pesados. 📱 Inclusão Total: Já incluído nativamente nesta versão em Componentes. ## ✅ Wrappers e Vortek A ponte entre o sistema Android e seus jogos. 🛠️ Gerenciamento Avançado: Inclusão do sistema de Wrappers e Vortek, similar ao encontrado no Winlator. 🔁 Seleção de Drivers: Permite escolher a camada de compatibilidade ideal para cada título. ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas Esta versão focou na arquitetura interna do aplicativo para suportar as novas funções de importação: 🔨 Refatoração do Gerenciador de Arquivos: Melhorias na leitura de caminhos locais e externos. 🛡️ Estabilidade em Importações: Tratamento de erros aprimorado ao ler arquivos .zip e .wcp ## 📥 Informações de Download Nome do Arquivo: GameHub-Brasil-v2.0.apk Requisitos Mínimos: Android 8.0 (Oreo) ou superior 4GB de RAM (Recomendado 6GB+ para uso do LowSpecsExperience) 3GB de espaço livre (Para instalação e drivers) GPU com suporte a Vulkan 1.1+ Dispositivo com suporte a OTG (Opcional, para armazenamento externo) ## 🙏 Agradecimentos Essa versão é um grande passo em direção à personalização. Agradecemos a todos os testadores que validaram as novas funções e algumas que foram importadas do Winlator Ludashi [StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi) E para você ficar por dentro do houve no app, segue o vídeo do nosso parceiro [BrunoAndroid](https://www.youtube.com/@BrunoAndroid) explicando sobre o funcionamento do app:

## 📞 Suporte e Feedback Encontrou algum bug na importação de drivers ou no Contents Hub? 🐛 Reporte problemas na aba [Issues](../../issues) 💡 Compartilhe seus perfis de controle e wrappers na comunidade 💡 Compartilhe ideias em [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@winlatorbrasil) ⭐ Se gostou da atualização, deixe uma estrela no repositório! --- ## 📥 Sobre os pacotes que você deve baixar | Posição (prioridade) | Nome / Categoria | Package name | Motivo de uso (performance / detecção) | |---------------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Futuremark | com.futuremark.dmandroid.application | Benchmark GPU moderno (Wild Life / Solar Bay), referência para modo gamer em vários aparelhos atuais, excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 2 | GFXBench | com.gfxbench.glbenchmark | Benchmark gráfico com cenários antigos e atuais (T-Rex, Manhattan, etc.), excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 3 | AnTuTu | com.antutu.ABenchMark | Benchmark geral muito popular (CPU + GPU + RAM + UX), frequentemente usado para liberar modo turbo em ROMs chinesas. | | 4 | Ludashi | com.ludashi.benchmark | Benchmark chinês focado em score geral e otimização, conhecido por acionar perfis especiais em alguns devices e ROMs custom. | | 5 | Redmagic | com.riotgames.league.wildrift | Usado por fabricantes para ativar modos de alta performance e alta taxa de quadros. | | 6 | MiHoYo | com.mihoyo.yuanshen | Usado como “teste real” de SoC e gatilho de perfil gamer. | | 7 | PUBG | com.tencent.ig | Tradicionalmente incluído em listas de otimização para reduzir lag e melhorar estabilidade de FPS. | | 8 | GameHub Brasil | com.gamehub.brasil | App genérico/launcher para cenário “normal”, útil para manter um perfil padrão sem overclock ou boost ativado. | ---
**Desenvolvido com ❤️ para a comunidade gaming brasileira** *Jogue mais, jogue melhor!* **V2.0: Mais liberdade, mais jogos!**

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v1.0
releases.body:
# 🎮 GameHub Brasil v1.0 🇧🇷

--- ## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão Esta versão representa um marco significativo em otimização e estabilidade, trazendo melhorias substanciais de performance para todos os chipsets suportados, além de uma experiência visual completamente renovada e novos componentes de emulação de última geração. --- ## 📦 Novos Componentes e Drivers ### **Box64** - Camada de Tradução x86_64 Atualizações importantes para melhor compatibilidade e performance: - ✨ **Box64 0.3.8** - Versão estável com correções críticas - 🚀 **Box64 0.3.9-d730610** - Build de desenvolvimento com otimizações avançadas - ⚡ **WowBox64 0.3.9-d730610** - Variante otimizada para gaming ### **DXVK** - Camada de Tradução DirectX para Vulkan Nova série com GPLAsync e Link-Lazy para máxima performance: - 🎯 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.6.3-1** - Base estável com async pipeline - 🔧 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.6.4-1** - Correções e melhorias incrementais - 💎 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.7.1-1** - Versão mais recente com otimizações de shader ### **FEX-Emu** - Emulador x86/x86_64 - 🆕 **FEXCore-2510** - Última geração do núcleo de emulação com melhorias significativas de velocidade ### **Turnip** - Driver Vulkan Mesa para Adreno Suporte completo ao Mesa 26.0.0: - 🔥 **Turnip v26.0.0 R1 Autotuner** - Ajuste automático de performance baseado em GPU - ⚙️ **Turnip v26.0.0 R1** - Versão padrão otimizada --- ## ⚡ Otimizações de Performance por Chipset Esta versão inclui ajustes específicos para extrair o máximo desempenho de cada arquitetura: ### 🔥 **Snapdragon (Qualcomm)** - Otimizações para GPUs Adreno - Perfis customizados para séries 6xx, 7xx e 8xx - Melhor aproveitamento de cache L2/L3 ### 🚀 **Dimensity / Helio (MediaTek)** - Ajustes para arquitetura ARM Mali - Balanceamento de carga entre clusters CPU - Otimização de thermal throttling ### 💫 **Exynos (Samsung)** - Perfis específicos para Mali e Xclipse - Gerenciamento inteligente de frequências - Compatibilidade aprimorada com drivers --- ## 🎨 Renovação Visual Completa ### **Interface Modernizada** - 🖼️ **Novo Wallpaper Principal** - Design moderno e imersivo - 🎬 **Intro Redesenhada** - Animação de inicialização atualizada - 🎨 **Paleta de Cores Renovada** - Esquema cromático mais harmônico e profissional - 🌈 **Wallpapers Adicionais** - Nova coleção de papéis de parede temáticos ### **Seção "Sobre" Aprimorada** - 📝 HTML customizado em "Informações Pessoais" - 💼 Layout mais profissional e organizado - ℹ️ Informações atualizadas e detalhadas --- ## 🌐 Novos Recursos ### **Feed de Notícias & Jogos Grátis** - 📰 Fique atualizado com as últimas notícias do mundo dos games - 🎁 Descubra jogos gratuitos disponíveis - 🔔 Sistema de notificações para novos conteúdos ### **Tradução PT-BR Automática** - 🇧🇷 Interface completamente em Português do Brasil - 🔤 Tradução inteligente de conteúdos dinâmicos --- ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas e Correções ### **Otimização de Conectividade** - ❌ **Removida integração direta com GitHub** - Solução definitiva para erro "429: Too Many Requests" - Eliminação de problemas de carregamento do menu ### **Estabilidade Geral** - 🛡️ Correções de bugs críticos - ⚙️ Gerenciamento de recursos otimizado --- ## 📥 Informações de Download **Nome do Arquivo:** `GameHub-Brasil-v1.0.apk` **Requisitos Mínimos:** - Android 8.0 (Oreo) ou superior - 2GB de RAM (4GB recomendado) - 500MB de espaço livre - GPU com suporte a Vulkan 1.1+ --- ## 🙏 Agradecimentos Agradecemos a toda comunidade GameHub Brasil pelo feedback contínuo e suporte. Esta versão é resultado direto das sugestões e relatórios de vocês! E ao GameHub Lite 🤜🤛. --- ## 📞 Suporte e Feedback Encontrou algum problema ou tem sugestões? - 🐛 Reporte bugs na aba [Issues](../../issues) - 💡 Compartilhe ideias em [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@winlatorbrasil) - ⭐ Se gostou, deixe uma estrela no repositório! ---

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V2-hotfix
releases.body:
Changelog:
• Ported SD Card support from recent [GameHub 5.3.3 Release Candidate](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3-RC1) releases
• Merged two APKs into one - added API switch into settings, by default it's set to EmuReady API, can be switched to official GameHub API, restart the app after changing API

Hotfix:
• Included Documents Provider patch (access to /data/data without root)

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V1
releases.body:
Ported [GameHub Lite 5.3.3](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3) modification to 5.3.5, two versions included, general changes:
- Disabled Discover and Free games tabs (as they don't work in both versions)
- DocumentsProvider patch included (app's /data/ can be accessed through file managers)
- Preserved original GameHub resources (icon and stuff, lazy to replace)
- Used package name - emuready.gamehub.lite
- Signed with testkey

Official API version:
- Uses GameSir server for downloading firmware/drivers/other components, usually up to date, may have privacy concerns
- More cluttered My Games list, includes PC/PS Link (idk if it is working)
- Can import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

EmuReady API version:
- Uses EmuReady server to download firmware/drivers/other components, more private
- Has more GPU drivers to download, but may not have the latest components to download
- Clean My Games list (only Windows emulation and Steam)
- Can't import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

Only difference in APK files are https server strings, everything else is the same!
